hook.headline: Three AI IDEs that actually matter in July 2026.

cards.label: 01 · cursor
cards.title: Cursor 2 = the polished default.
cards.body: Strongest agent + chat hybrid. Biggest community of shared rules and workflows. If you switch nothing else for the rest of 2026, switch to Cursor 2 and don't look back.

cards.label: 02 · windsurf
cards.title: Windsurf = the gentler ramp.
cards.body: Cascade agent asks before doing anything. Cleaner UI than Cursor. $5/mo cheaper. Best pick if you're new to AI-assisted coding and don't want the AI making moves you didn't approve.

cards.label: 03 · claude-code
cards.title: Claude Code = pure terminal.
cards.body: No GUI, no extensions, no electron. Open a terminal, type `claude`, talk to your repo. For devs who already live in tmux + vim and don't want a third VS Code instance eating their RAM.

cards.label: 04 · pick
cards.title: When to pick which.
cards.body: Daily heavy use → Cursor 2. New to AI coding → Windsurf. Terminal native + want to script agents into CI → Claude Code. All three use the same underlying models — the difference is workflow ergonomics.

cards.label: 05 · india
cards.title: Cheapest for solo devs in India.
cards.body: Claude Code: BYO key, ~₹2-4k/mo for typical use. Cursor Pro: ₹1,700/mo flat. Windsurf Pro: ₹1,250/mo. Windsurf wins on predictable cost. Claude Code wins on pay-as-you-go.

take.use_if: You're picking your first (or next) AI IDE in mid-2026 — go Cursor 2 unless you have a specific reason.
take.skip_if: You've already got an opinionated setup that works — switching IDEs costs 2 weeks of ergonomic relearning.
